key_specs.Display: 50-inch Class (49.5" viewable) UHD 4K LED Panel, 3840x2160 resolution
key_specs.Panel_Type: Direct LED backlight LCD (no local dimming)
key_specs.Smart_Platform: webOS 24 with LG ThinQ, LG Channels (300+ free channels), FILMMAKER MODE
key_specs.Connectivity: 3x HDMI 2.0, 2x USB-A, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, Ethernet; ALLM & HGiG gaming mode support; no HDMI 2.1
key_specs.Voice_Assistants: Amazon Alexa, Google Assistant, Apple AirPlay 2 & HomeKit

strengths: Solid 4K UHD resolution at a budget-friendly price point (~$299–$329 street)
webOS 24 smart platform is polished and easy to navigate
300+ free LG Channels included out of the box
α5 AI Gen7 processor handles upscaling and virtual 5.1-ch audio up-mix
FILMMAKER MODE for director-intended picture accuracy
LG Game Optimizer hub for quick gaming settings; ALLM & HGiG support
Includes Magic Remote MR24 in box
Works with Alexa, Google Assistant, AirPlay 2, and HomeKit
Slim, minimal-bezel design
weaknesses: Only 60 Hz native refresh rate — no 120 Hz support
No local dimming, limiting contrast and black levels
No HDMI 2.1 ports (all HDMI 2.0), limiting 4K/120Hz gaming from PS5/Xbox Series X
No Dolby Vision HDR — limited to HDR10 and HLG only
Entry-level panel brightness unlikely to excel in bright rooms
No optical audio output noted (may limit legacy surround sound connections)

TCL.model: TCL 50S551G (S5-Series, 50" — closest verified match to '50S5'; no discrete model '50S5' found)

key_specs.HDR Support: HDR PRO+ — Dolby Vision, HDR10+, HDR10, HLG
key_specs.Refresh Rate / Motion: Motion Rate 240 with MEMC frame insertion (native panel rate not officially disclosed)
key_specs.Gaming: Game Accelerator 120 with VRR (up to 120Hz VRR), Auto Game Mode (ALLM)
key_specs.Audio: Dolby Atmos, DTS Virtual:X, Enhanced Dialogue Mode

strengths: Comprehensive HDR format support (Dolby Vision, HDR10+, HDR10, HLG) at an entry-level price
Google TV smart platform with voice remote, broad app ecosystem, and AirPlay 2 compatibility
VRR and ALLM support (Game Accelerator 120) make it capable for casual gaming
MEMC-based motion enhancement (Motion Rate 240) for smoother sports and action
Strong value proposition at ~$250 for a 50" 4K set
TCL AIPQ Processor with deep-learning AI for scene-by-scene picture optimization
Dolby Atmos and DTS Virtual:X spatial audio from built-in speakers
weaknesses: LED edge-lit panel — no local dimming zones disclosed, limiting contrast vs. QLED/Mini-LED competitors
Native refresh rate not officially confirmed; 'Motion Rate 240' is a marketing figure
No QLED/wide color gamut technology (unlike step-up Q5/Q6 series at similar price points)
Built-in speaker output likely modest (exact wattage not confirmed in available specs)
Remote battery life complaints noted in user reviews
Peak brightness figures not officially published, limiting HDR highlight performance assessment

strengths: Very competitive entry-level price for a 50" 4K TV
Google TV smart platform with broad app ecosystem and voice control
Dolby Vision and HDR10+ support at this price tier is above average
HDMI 2.1 ports with VRR and ALLM (Auto Low Latency Mode) for gaming
FilmMaker Mode for accurate movie reproduction
AI 4K Upscaler for non-native 4K content
DTS Virtual:X simulated surround sound
weaknesses: Entry-level VA panel with modest brightness (~300 nits), limiting HDR peak impact
No local dimming, so black levels are limited for an LCD
60 Hz native panel — no 120 Hz support for high-frame-rate gaming
Limited built-in audio power (14W total); external soundbar recommended
No QLED or quantum dot color enhancement
Narrower viewing angles than IPS panels despite VA technology
